<?php declare(strict_types=1); namespace Brick\Math\Exception; use function dechex; use function ord; use function sprintf; use function strtoupper; /** * Exception thrown when attempting to create a number from a string with an invalid format. */ final class NumberFormatException extends MathException { /** * @pure */ public static function invalidFormat(string $value): self { return new self(sprintf( 'The given value "%s" does not represent a valid number.', $value, )); } /** * @param string $char The failing character. * * @pure */ public static function charNotInAlphabet(string $char): self { return new self(sprintf( 'Character %s is not valid in the given alphabet.', self::charToString($char), )); } /** * @pure */ private static function charToString(string $char): string { $ord = ord($char); if ($ord < 32 || $ord > 126) { $char = strtoupper(dechex($ord)); if ($ord < 16) { $char = '0' . $char; } return '0x' . $char; } return '"' . $char . '"'; } }
